finding_results.notes: Rebuilt all four cited reveal beats. Vess's sister-name beat (line 127) now shows her attempting phonetic retrieval — trying sounds, stopping, restarting — before landing on a flat operational statement instead of describing the shape of the absence. Vess's anger beat (lines 143, 147) now documents the missing emotion through contractual evidence (risk-factor flag, below-rate fee) rather than the room/door metaphor chain; her landing is one operational sentence ('a reason I can document and can't feel'). Aldric's gap beat (line 207) now shows him attempting to reconstruct what he saw — reaching for 'structures, lines' — failing, then falling back to precise first-chamber data (dimensions, contamination readings) as concrete contrast with the missing second-chamber understanding; abstract landing compressed to 'clean edge, nothing past it.' Aldric's elaboration (line 211) compressed from three thesis sentences to one brief diagnostic ('The edge is too clean. Something was there, and it was taken.'). Voice separation maintained: Vess uses contract terms, risk factors, and operational shorthand; Aldric uses chamber measurements, contamination classifications, and uncontracted formal syntax. No character exceeds one abstract/metaphor sentence in the revised beats.

summary: Single finding addressed. Replaced polished thesis-speech in both fragment reveal beats with failed-retrieval attempts and concrete operational/classificatory residue. Vess now tries phonetic sounds for the missing name and cites contract documentation as proof of unfelt anger. Aldric now attempts to reconstruct wall patterns and falls back to precise chamber data he can still access. Extended metaphor chains (room/door, emptied/negative-space) removed; each fragment gets at most one brief abstract landing. Voice separation sharpened through vocabulary domain: Vess stays contractual and operational, Aldric stays classificatory and formal. All must-land beats, state deltas, and open hooks preserved.
